reviews.review_description: It didn't let me vote one star

i mean i literally cried. enough said 
. please watch this for yourself
I remember I bought this game because we had a project in writing class for Black History Month and I played this game to understand everything! this game has a lot of potential!

MLK: Now Is The Time” is an immersive virtual reality experience created by TIME Studios, and it explores the enduring relevance of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.'s “I Have a Dream” speech. It focuses on the systemic injustices he highlighted, such as those in housing, voting, and policing. The project uses VR to make King’s words tangible, allowing users to interact with scenarios that illustrate ongoing racial inequalities.
The experience is both powerful and thought-provoking, though some users have noted that the visuals can feel a bit uncanny. Overall, it serves as a poignant reminder that the issues Dr. King spoke about are still very much present today.
I shouldve left a review long ago. This is truly powerful and beautiful to experience.We need more like this.

reviews.review_title: Mysterious fluid leak, a bug in the app?
reviews.review_description: Unexplainable fluids are leaking from my device while viewing this.  The fluid is running down my eyes, down my cheek.   I can't figure out what the fluid is or where it comes out from.   The bug is in this app because other apps do not cause this mysterious fluid leak.

It’s wonderful to listen to clips of Dr. MLK, Jr.’s I Have a Dream speech. It would have been nice if I was immersed within the crowd as if I was in attendance that day in 1963. The floating red and yellow balls are distracting and meaningless. Some optics are too high!  The board game would have been nice to show more options but demonstrates the point-you cannot win! The driving experience doesn’t adapt when you remove your hands off the wheel. More interactive experiences would enhance this VR.

reviews.review_title: I can't do it.. I tried
reviews.review_description: In the beginning of it was a bit scary and okay, until I got to the scene of being in the police car. I'm a brown sista( I don't like to be called black) that scene was scary. I have PTSD, I tried to sit through it until the officer said "what are you reaching for". I snatched the head set off. I believe I can't do that scene because I've been held up at gun point 8 years ago when I use to manage a restaurant.me and my husband did.. I just can't do it.. I know we as a brown skin community need to know what to do when we get pulled over.. but that was just too intense. Please do some changes. I'm uninstalling the app.

reviews.review_title: It's done well but.... It's a lesson on
reviews.review_description:
How to diminish what Dr Martian Luther King stood for. The Black Fist/ Soviet Red salute, really???               
I feel like I'm watching a poorly written episode from MSNBC. You can tell the whole thing was written by white guilt laden Karen's and soy's. Lol. You want every stereotype , it's got you covered. 
I went into it feeling all festive because of MLK day, and I had to turn it off about half way through, again because of stereotypical situations. You can tell the writers want everyone to feel bad, but it started to come off as a comedy sketch. It actually wants you to believe the black people are schizophrenic and psychotic believing in things that just aren't true. There are gullible people out there, so hearts and minds may be won on them, but I think the majority will see right through this wholesomeness wrapped propaganda.  Watch it for the lolz.
I can’t get pass the opening screen. It tells me I need the hand tracking, which I have but still unable to interact with stone.

developer_response.body:
Sorry you're having this problem! 

If you're having trouble getting hand tracking to work, first ensure that the option is enabled: under "Settings -> Device-> Hands and Controllers" make sure the "Hand Tracking" toggle is set to "On". We recommend also setting "Auto Switch Between Hands and Controllers" to "On", as well, so you can switch between input modes seamlessly, depending on the application you're using. 

If these are set correctly, and you're still not getting consistent recognition, ensure that the hand gestures are being made in view of the headset cameras. They're generally most accurately tracked in front of you, in the area where you're looking/your head is pointed.

If this is all working, and you're still having trouble grabbing the stone to begin the experience please let us know, and we can help troubleshoot further.

reviews.review_description: Disappointing to see MLK used to push a partisan viewpoint that does not address any progress... give anyone hope... and even forces you to fail.
Kudos to the developers and writers for the immersive and interactive nature of this history.

The point, I believe, is to convey the depth of the social, political, and financial systemic forces designed to keep Black Americans controlled, oppressed, and in impoverished. Many today don't have a clue as to how difficult and dangerous the situation was and the courage of Rev. Dr. King and thousands more who stood up against those systems. These are not stereotypes but realities of Black life. For that the app was great and it is not a "game" so don't go into it looking to have game-type "fun". It is a look back at the "mountain of despair" that we have overcome.
The hand tracking, however, is problematic. I do not know if hand-tracking is not ready for prime time or if the app's implementation is less than ideal.  When you raise your fist - you can see the virtual fist so you know hand-tracking is working...  but there's no real indication (feed back) that you are interacting with the app (no buttons to push, so you don't know what the response should be when your fist is raised)  so when nothing happens you are left wondering is it the app? the headset, the hand-tracking? Is my shoe untied?  App needs to combine the raising of the fist with definitive  tactile, visual, and/or audible feedback...
This was very informative and interactive a little short but very enjoyable thank for this app.
Everyone should see this.  This was put together well.  So much information.
I haven’t played it yet because I am waiting for the actual Martin Luther King day because it’s based on Martin Luther King
This was well put together. I hope the developers do a longer, more detailed version.

reviews.review_title: The best educational app on occulus
reviews.review_description: To be emerged in to the culture of black america is like no other this was an awesome experience and I suggest every single person invest the the few minutes of your time into this.
